title: Valuation Advisory Manager
description:
Grant Thornton is a global professional service network of more than 73,000 people in 150 markets. Our team in Singapore is growing rapidly – and we know this success is made possible by our people. That’s why we make sure our people have the right tools and environment to thrive.

Each individual at Grant Thornton Singapore is empowered to be themselves within an inclusive team. Our supportive structure gives our people the autonomy to shape their careers and drive positive change the firm. It’s an exciting time to join us as we grow with organisations of all sizes and industries to create change in Singapore, across the region and in the world.

The Valuation team

Our Valuation team blends technical expertise with a pragmatic outlook to deliver support during the financial reporting process, transactions, restructuring and disputes. The Valuations team creates meaningful value tailored to the needs of each client and is agile and adaptable to market developments.

Responsibilities

  • Perform valuations for businesses, intangible assets, financial instruments, etc., for the purpose of financial reporting, transactions, and litigation support
  • Conduct market and industry research, construct and analyse financial models, and ensure best practices and quality in valuation work
  • Support in pitching new projects, including preparation of proposals, engagement letters, marketing materials, and technical documents
  • Perform purchase price allocations for post-transaction financial reporting purposes.
  • Prepare comprehensive valuation reports and review memos which meet the requirements of internal and external clients.
  • Assist in client management, practice development, and business development activities
  • Build and maintain client relationships before, during, and after engagements
  • Mentor and conduct training sessions for team member

Qualifications and skills

  • At least 5 years of relevant experience in valuation advisory in a professional services firm
  • Recognised degree in Accounting, Finance, Business or other related fields or equivalent professional qualifications
  • Familiar with Singapore Financial Reporting Standards, IFRS and US GAAP
  • Strong written and verbal communication and presentation skills
  • Good understanding of business valuation concepts and the ability to analyse situations and recommend valuation approaches and next steps
  • Familiarity with key valuation methodologies
  • Strong work ethic and ability to adapt to new challenges in a collaborative team environment
  • Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to prioritise client engagements

Grant Thornton is a global professional service network of more than 73,000 people in 150 markets. Our team in Singapore is growing rapidly and we know this success is made possible by our people. That’s why we make sure our people have the right tools and environment to thrive.

Each individual at Grant Thornton Singapore is empowered to be themselves within an inclusive team. Our supportive structure gives our people the autonomy to shape their careers and drive positive change in the firm. It’s an exciting time to join us as we grow with organisations of all sizes and industries in Singapore, across the region and in the world.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity firm where we celebrate what makes our employees unique. We are committed to nurturing our inclusive workplace and strongly encourage people from all backgrounds and walks of life to discover more about us.
